seo.title: N. Carolina Dental Assistant License (2026) | Fees & Guide
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in North Carolina — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license north carolina, dental assistant certification north carolina, RDA north carolina, DANB CDA north carolina, dental assistant radiography north carolina

board.name: North Carolina State Board of Dental Examiners

quick_facts.exam_name: National Entry Level Dental Assistant (NELDA) exam, or the Certified Dental Assistant (CDA) exam, or successful completion of a CODA-accredited dental assisting program

license_types.title: Dental Assistant (classification based)
license_types.description: No state-level credential required for general dental assistants in North Carolina.

steps.title: Review Registration Requirements
steps.description: Review the specific requirements for the desired Dental Assistant registration type (e.g., DA I, DA II) on the North Carolina State Board of Dental Examiners (NCSBDE) website.

steps.title: Prepare Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Gather all required documents such as current CPR certification, approved dental radiography course completion, and any other relevant credentials as outlined by the NCSBDE for your chosen registration type.

steps.title: Create Account on NCSBDE Online Services
steps.description: Navigate to the NCSBDE Online Services portal at https://ncdentalboard.org/OnlineServices/ and create a new account or log in if you are an existing user.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Access the Dental Assistant Registration application within the NCSBDE Online Services portal, fill out all required sections accurately, and upload all supporting documents electronically.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the required application fee through the NCSBDE Online Services portal to finalize your application submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Track the status of your application through the NCSBDE Online Services portal and respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Board.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: North Carolina does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. DANB national certification is recognized and recommended.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: No, North Carolina does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in North Carolina.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in North Carolina?
faqs.answer: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in North Carolina. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.

reciprocity.notes: North Carolina classifies dental assistants (DA I, DA II, DA III) based on experience and training rather than formal licensure.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in North Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for Dental Assistant registration in North Carolina, new applicants must create an account and submit their application through the NCSBDE Online Services portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the NCSBDE Online Services portal, click 'Create Account' for new users or 'Login' for existing users, and follow the prompts to register your profile before starting your Dental Assistant registration application.
